Hello David,  Do you know someone who would like to volunteer to add their mother tongue or hand signs to the His Hands Reader literacy system?  The Www.HisHandsReader.org mother tongue literacy videos with hand sign assist open a path for total inclusion of deaf children among hearing.  As hearing children use the mother tongue literacy videos with hand sign assist they naturally learn the means to communicate with deaf playmates.  And don't  over look that the Www.HisHandsReader.org mother tongue literacy videos help Hearing children in the Multi lingual Education classroom.  It takes only 30 hours to add your mother tongue.  Here are the instructions to volunteer... http://www.hishandsreader.org/add-my-language-odk-collect.html  Here is an example of the result of volunteers in Jordan who added their local business and mother tongue languages and local hand signs... http://www.hishandsreader.org/1jo-jos-arb-ajp.html  Thanks!  Bob Achgill Www.HisHandsReader.org Helping Deaf and Hearing learn to read so they can read His Word  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Here is a little more detail...  Would you like to volunteer to add your hand signs or mother tongue to Www.HisHandsReader.org??  Here are the ONLY qualifications needed to add your Mother Tongue language or hand signs to the Www.HisHandsReader.org : 1) Native speaker of your mother tongue or hand signs. 2) Read and write your mother tongue (not needed for adding hand signs) 3) Know English well enough to translate the 1742 basic Words to your MT or hand signs 4) Access to Android phone to use the HHR app to add your language. (1.5GB RAM, 3 GB free space) 5) total 30 hours to add your text with voice or video your hand signs.

Dear David:    So sorry for the delay!  My mind has been all over the place.  Thanks for your interest in my new job and company.  I've know the principals in this group for more than 20 years.  Sort of like family, so I was cautious about joining and ruining a good thing.  The time seemed right this time around.  My role is external marketing, largely social media.  I am also helping to create a new member's only site to assist small businesses and non-profits.      Tom continues full-time work at Wycliffe in Government and Community Relations.  I support him as needed and have also started giving time to the children/youth programs through the Discovery Center. Thank you for the invitation to join your network. How are you? As you can see from my profile, I am now working with Lutheran Bible Translators. Ruth and I felt challenged by God to get more Lutherans involved in Bible translation work. This means we had to move to the Chicago area. It is cold here half the year! But we know we re where God has called us.    Right now I am in Ethiopia visiting the Ethiopian Evangelical Church Mekane Yesus. They invited us to come and partner with them in Southwest Ethiopia.    How far along is your translation now? One question I keep asking myself when I visit places is, "Who is able to touch and change lives with the Word of God, and how do they do it?" I met a pastor in Ghana who is training 20 lay evangelists. He translated the New Testament into h is languge, and they are using that. He is also writing a book of liturgy and prayers that they can use to help them with things like funerals, sickness, births, harvest festivals, and other special occasions. He has also translated the catechism for them to use to train others in the basics of the faith. This local man seems to be always looking for ways to touch his people's lives with the Truth!    Blessings,  David

About a year ago, with the blessing of the senior leadership of Wycliffe USA, we moved from Wycliffe to Lutheran Bible Translators.     While we don't like the cold of Chicago <smile>, we do feel we are at the right place now. It is different - much, much smaller. But there is much possibility for growth and impact here. Dear Carl, I hope this serves you well. I certainly appreciated our time together. And I also valued the input your wife gave!!    Keep up the good work. Don't avoid conflict, but let it continue to advance the kingdom of God.    Miss you all,  David  Details of the Recommendation:  "I value Carl. He is a hard working, intelligent, and listening person.     During my five years as Vice President for Personnel with Wycliffe USA, Carl served in several key roles within my department. He lead a team of trainers and coaches to completely redesign a major component of Wycliffe's orientation for all new missionary staff. He did an excellent job at this, producing a program that was well received by participants and proved to be very effective for the organization.    During this time Carl also lead a smaller team the provided career guidance counseling services to Wycliffe's staff. Here again Carl brought professionalism to the job. As in his other roles, he regularly participated in personal professional skill development, and encouraged his staff to do the same.    There were a few occasions where Carl and I had disagreements on the best way forward. In each occasion, we were able to work through the disagreement and come to a better position for us and for the organization. I value staff who are willing to voice a strongly held position, yet equally willing to work through a difference in order to accomplish our shared goals. I grew through my interactions with Carl, and the organization certainly benefited from those conflicts.    I do not hesitate to endorse Carl Campbell."

CONVERSATION TITLE: Join Wharton's Exec Ed's Chief Strategy Officer Program
CONTENT:
Hi David,


My name is Eric Hamberger, Managing Director at Wharton Online. I am reaching out to you because, as a senior strategy executive, your profile is perfectly aligned with our Chief Strategy Officer (CSO) Program. 


I would like to invite you to the Chief Strategy Officer (CSO) Program which is suited for senior executives aiming to understand external environments, read early warning signals of disruption, and align the organization’s strategy to gain a competitive advantage.


The CSO Program offers an unique opportunity to catalyze your personal growth and step into global leadership roles. Leveraging the wisdom of Wharton’s distinguished faculty, you will gain the confidence to tackle disruptive forces and evolve into an influential strategic architect capable of navigating business challenges with ease. 


Upon successful completion of the program, you will be able to:

  • Evaluate an organization's strategies and market strengths to identify improvements and competitive advantages.
  • Analyze an organization's readiness for transformative change and the foundation needed for sustainable long-term growth.
  • Apply proven approaches and models to facilitate successful expansion into foreign markets and industries
  • Identify and incorporate disruptive tech trends to drive competitive advantage and uncover transformational opportunities.
  • Apply effective frameworks and key metrics to evaluate an organization's readiness to scale its operations. 
  • Examine key leadership qualities and organizational factors needed to execute strategic initiatives and foster innovation and collaboration.


Through a blend of cutting-edge core modules and short courses, topical case studies,

a capstone project, and try-it activities, the program will enable you to hone your strategic acumen and lead strategy in your organization. 


The program offers an optional two-day networking event at the University of Pennsylvania campus in Philadelphia. Meet multiple cohort peers, industry experts, and faculty to forge lifelong connections. 


Following successful completion of the CSO Program and subsequent open-enrollment programs, subject to further qualification criteria and additional tuition, you will also have a pathway to apply for Wharton alumni status.



Download the brochure to learn more about the program! 

Best Regards, 

Eric Hamberger

Dear David,


Are your finances out of control? When it comes to figuring out how to take charge of your money, you’re not alone. At MMBB, we understand the struggle of pastors whose income may not meet their living expenses. We know financial challenges can distract you from the demands of ministry. When you can manage your financial decisions, you can focus on caring for your congregation and community. MMBB is here to help.


How to Begin?

Start by creating a budget. Identify all your current income sources, including salaries, honoraria, and if you’re retired, social security. Next, pinpoint your regular expenses.


Required vs. Discretionary Costs

One key to controlling your spending is to differentiate between required and discretionary expenses. Required costs (housing, utilities, food) must be paid. While discretionary expenses (movie tickets) are optional. If cash flow is tight, look for ways to cut discretionary costs.


Balancing Your Budget

Once you have identified your income and created a budget, set realistic goals. Review your spending and saving monthly. If you haven’t met your goals, you may need another source of income, like a second job. Once your budget is on track, you can focus on planning, saving and decreasing debt.


To learn more, click the link below and fill out the “Contact Us” form. Guiding you towards financial peace of mind is central to our ministry.         


Sincerely,

MMBB
